diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Civil Law from Roman law to modern codifications. Key milestones: the Twelve Tables (450 BCE) as the first written Roman law; Justinian's Corpus Juris Civilis (529‑534 CE) systematizing Roman jurisprudence; the rediscovery of Roman law in medieval universities (Bologna, 11th century); the influence of canon law; the French Civil Code (Code Napoléon, 1804) marking modern codification; the Austrian ABGB (1811); the German Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB, 1900); the Swiss Civil Code (1907); the spread of civil law systems through colonization and legal transplants; and contemporary developments such as European harmonization of private law (PECL, DCFR).
